channel.title: Hadosh Academy Blog
channel.description: Articles on agent engineering, AI architecture, and the future of building cognitive systems.

item.title: LLMs Are Not the Agents
item.description: Most people point at the model and call it the agent. That is the root misunderstanding. The agent is the filesystem — the local files that give an LLM memory, structure, and identity. The LLM is just the engine.

item.title: We Could Have Had AGI By Now
item.description: The building blocks for durable, self-improving agents have been available for months. If we had scaled architecture instead of scaling only the model, we could have had AGI-like autonomy already.

item.title: Your Brain Was Never Built for This
item.description: Your skull holds a brain optimized for spotting snakes in tall grass. You are asking it to run a business in a civilization of eight billion people. CLI agents are the digital cognitive organs your biology never built.

item.title: The Folder Is Alive
item.description: Add a .claude/ directory to any folder, and that folder becomes an agent. Twenty such folders, taught one job at a time, become a personal cognitive workforce — your liver, but for paperwork.

item.title: The Language of Agents
item.description: Every industry builds a wall of jargon. AI built one faster than any industry in history. This post hands you the keys — from LLMs to seed agents, one read, every word that matters.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 1: The Two-Layer Foundation
item.description: The seed agent's two plugin categories — always-on and phasic — over one shared CLAUDE.md substrate. Single-concern + careful coupling lets the parts compose into ceremonies. Opens the eight-part Always-On Digital Cortex series.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 2: Plugin Edit Safety (plugin_integrity)
item.description: The test gate underneath every plugin code change. Pass and commit, fail and revert. The universal floor most architects inherit rather than rewrite — but the inside of the gate has knobs.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 3: Context Window Discipline (brain_guard)
item.description: The progressive squeeze. Three tiers tighten as the window fills until self-compaction is the only graceful move. Shape-compels-production via the five-section /compact template. Tier positions are tunable per model.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 4: Job Lifecycle (job_core)
item.description: The agent's unit of compartmentalization. Cumulative mega-prompt, shared-key cross-plugin extension, phase-aware refusal to stop, staged completion. Re-shape the noun for your domain.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 5: Cross-Session Memory (interaction_summary)
item.description: Block-until-summarized. Five-section template forces shape-compels-production on the job's growing mega-prompt. Threshold + sections are this prototype's choice; the shape is the lesson.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 6: Structured Questions (question_discipline)
item.description: Every question the seed agent asks the user carries a registered prefix. Nine ceremonies in the prototype registry. Batch-cascade validation; subagent bypass. Extend the registry as your seed grows new ceremonies.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 7: The CLAUDE.md Hierarchy
item.description: The substrate underneath the always-on layer. CLAUDE.md as native primitive, partitioned into a body and four phase footers that inflate across OPEVC and deflate at CONDENSE. The altered list scopes execution.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 8: The Historian Ratchet
item.description: Per-plugin living history. Drift counter climbs with commits, blocks unlock at the threshold, historian re-narrates evolution.md, counter resets. Three plugins compose into one ceremony — no single plugin owns the whole thing.

item.title: The Always-On Digital Cortex — Pt 9: The Customization Guardrail
item.description: The gate that decides when substrate edits are admitted at all. PLUGIN-LOCK admits on gmode OR a user-approved job. The propose-and-confirm flow via [JOB-APPROVE-CREATION]. The two-route design and why both routes exist.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 1: Phasic Foundation
item.description: Phases are cognitive isolation chambers. Five phases plus one cognitive organ — observe, plan, execute, verify, condense. Opens the nine-part Markov Phasic Brain series.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 2: The Discipline and the Map
item.description: Forbidding tools is the pedagogy. The per-phase write rule, the altered list scoping, and the quick map of the cycle.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 3: OBSERVE: Read Wide, Write Once
item.description: The intake phase. Read-only on project files; synthesis goes into CLAUDE.md. Multiplier sentinel, parallel research subagents, point system underneath.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 4: PLAN: Decide, Then Lock
item.description: Read-only. The phase that names the plan file, decides, and locks the contract that execute will materialize.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 5: EXECUTE: Build, in Scope, in Steps
item.description: The build phase. Full write access, scoped to the altered list, with multi-commit checkpoints. Where the plan becomes artifacts.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 6: VERIFY: Independent Eyes
item.description: The audit chamber. Scripts only, auditor subagents, multi-backward routing. The cycle's final guardrail before CONDENSE absorbs the work.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 7: CONDENSE: The Cognitive Organ
item.description: The phase that acts on the brain. Seven-step waterfall routes the cycle's experiential data into durable memory. The deflation gate enforces compression. CONDENSE is the only phase allowed to edit the brain itself.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 7b: CONDENSE: What It Uniquely Owns
item.description: What CONDENSE uniquely owns beyond the waterfall: the job graph it alone may grow, the three VERIFY dependency-removal moves, and the reflection pass that closes the phase and seals the cycle's learning.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 8: The Rhythm of Work
item.description: The count-based rhythm inside every phase. The min-max gate forces read-before-write and synthesize-before-reading-more, counted per activity class; a CLAUDE.md update resets the board. The boundary opens on evidence of reflection — the three-family exit gate — and every gate is a floor, not a target.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 9: GMODE: The Off-Cycle Lane
item.description: The operator's deliberate maintenance lane that lives outside the OPEVC cycle. Entered via [GMODE] with a ≥100-word justification.

item.title: The Markov Phasic Brain — Pt 10: The Plan File: Long-Horizon Memory
item.description: The multi-cycle plan-file lifecycle that scales the markov brain to long-horizon work. plan_state + the three job forms + .yaml injection at phase entry.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 1: Plugin Kit Foundation
item.description: The kit framing — every plugin is a cell with shared organs: CLAUDE.md, hooks, scripts, tests, data.json, docs, agents, voice.xml. Opens the nine-part Plugin Kit series.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 2: Skeleton: CLAUDE.md, Hooks, and Scripts
item.description: Three load-bearing organs — the markdown brain at the plugin root, the hook directory that fires on Claude Code events, and the scripts directory the hooks call.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 3: The Dual Voice Architecture
item.description: Why every plugin carries two voice.xml files — one in hooks/ that injects mid-conversation coaching, one in scripts/ that speaks at script-execution time. Same XML shape, two firing points.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 4: data.json — The Hidden State
item.description: The plugin's private runtime state — focused job, tier counter, summary chain, lock manifest. JSON, atomic-written, gitignored — never travels across machines.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 5: docs/ and the Historian
item.description: The narrated knowledge layer per plugin — principles, evolution, CLAUDE.md.template. The historian subagent ratchets evolution.md as the plugin matures.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 6: agents/ and the 80/20 Dispatch Budget
item.description: Each plugin's owned subagent pool with prefix-scoped naming (historian-*, observe-*, verify-*, condense-*). Main session orchestrates 20 percent; subagents execute 80 percent.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 7: Smaller Organs and Brain-Root Wiring
item.description: lib/, template/, MD/, archive/ — the smaller organs that round out the kit. How the brain root wires plugin hooks into settings.local.json.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 8: The Lock Ceremony
item.description: PLUGIN-LOCK + TEST-LOCK + safe-lock + historian compose into the ceremony every plugin code edit passes through. Pass and commit, fail and revert.

item.title: The Plugin Kit — Pt 9: Building a New Plugin
item.description: Two paths — sibling-extension via [PLUGIN-LOCK] for an established seed, or plugin-template clone for a from-scratch concern. Tier-3 architect work, end-to-end.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 1: Apprentice to Architect Foundation
item.description: The maturation arc the seed agent walks across months of work — three growth axes track the jobs, the plan-state machine, and the brain. Opens the nine-part From Apprentice to Architect series.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 2: The Stages of Job Maturation
item.description: The four stages a job moves through — single-cycle OPEVC, multi-cycle .md plan, multi-cycle .yaml plan, sealed. plan_state lives on the job, not in the file.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 3: What Lives in the Brain After Three Months
item.description: A precise inventory — what the prototype's brain holds today after three months of cycles. Small brain over a large knowledge layer with a narrow memory.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 4: Soft → Hard Migration
item.description: How a behavioral control travels from coaching voice to hardened hook to fossilized template. The mechanism that produces the small-brain / large-knowledge equilibrium.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 5: What's Enforced vs What's Discipline
item.description: The honest accounting. Which seed limits are hard caps, and which are discipline pretending to be hard? CONDENSE discipline is the major soft line that holds the brain at its ceiling.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 6: The Maturation Arc — Apprentice, Journeyman, Architect
item.description: The operator's parallel three-stage arc — apprentice, journeyman, architect — and the visible markers of each. The seed matures around you while you mature alongside it.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 7: The Brain Stops Growing in Size
item.description: The equilibrium claim — the brain reaches a ceiling while the knowledge layer never does. Growth becomes compression; learning continues, size does not.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 8: A System That Safely Modifies Itself
item.description: The Tier-3 close. The recursive lock ceremony — plugin_integrity guards itself the same way it guards every other plugin — and the rollback substrate that makes self-modification safe.

item.title: From Apprentice to Architect — Pt 9: The Seed Is Yours
item.description: The series closer. The seed agent is open-source under MIT; you clone it, run it, customize it through conversation, and the triangle of agent-developer-user collapses to agent-user.
